155709.explanation: It is already possible for residents to request a referendum. The CDA does not intend to abolish that possibility. However, the CDA preferably focuses on careful decision-making within the municipal council rather than organizing a referendum.

155710.explanation: If residents can perform a municipal task better than the municipality, the CDA finds it good if they take on that challenge. However, good agreements must be made, so that, for example, the maintenance level remains adequate.

155712.explanation: Taxes directly affect the livelihood security of families and households. Therefore, the CDA wants to keep property tax (ozb) as low as possible. If there is a shortage of funds to maintain facilities, the CDA does not want to place the burden on our residents but first critically examine municipal expenses.

155713.explanation: The CDA wants the municipality of Nijkerk to remain an independent municipality. Some tasks we cannot do entirely alone. Then we must cooperate. Think of household waste collection. Doing that independently is very expensive. Therefore, cooperation with other municipalities is sometimes necessary.

155714.explanation: The CDA wants the dog tax to be used exclusively for dog facilities, such as dog parks and cleaning dog walking areas. Abolishing it would mean that everyone’s property tax (ozb) would have to be raised to pay for these facilities. The CDA does not want that.

155715.explanation: Nijkerk is already a very popular location for companies, partly due to its central location. The CDA considers spending community money on a promotional campaign without real added value unwise. For establishing shops in the centers, the CDA considers it a good idea.

155716.explanation: The CDA values space for our entrepreneurs. Therefore, new work locations will be needed in the future. Space in our municipality is limited. Therefore, the CDA focuses on innovative work locations near public transport hubs and less on traditional industrial parks and large 'boxes'.

155717.explanation: When determining opening hours, the interests of the hospitality industry as well as those of nearby residents are important. Allowing the hospitality sector to set opening hours entirely independently goes a step too far for the CDA: the interests of nearby residents must also be taken into account.

155719.explanation: Sometimes a subsidy can be just the boost needed to combat energy poverty and help residents' sustainable initiatives succeed. Think of neighborhood batteries, insulation campaigns, or heat communities.

155721.explanation: Housing for status holders can be arranged in various ways. Housing in social rental homes is possible, but the CDA wants to look at this critically and also explore alternatives such as opening new transition and flexible locations currently funded by the national government.

155722.explanation: The CDA considers integration very important. This is not only a right but also a duty, in which the transmission of Dutch norms, values, and customs must be central to the integration process.

155725.explanation: The CDA embraces it when residents take responsibility for their neighborhood. If a good plan needs a little support, the CDA is happy to help with municipal assistance or a contribution.

155728.explanation: The CDA wants to raise the threshold for drug use, with education aimed at youth and a firm approach to drug-related crime. Allowing coffee shops and low-threshold sales does not fit with this.

155730.explanation: The CDA wants to make safety a top priority. Camera surveillance can help prevent crime and also increase the chances of catching offenders. Therefore, the CDA is open to more camera surveillance where it helps combat crime.

155731.explanation: The CDA wants to support first-time buyers from our own municipality in purchasing their first home. The starter loan helps make the first home affordable. Therefore, we will maintain this scheme for starters from our own municipality.

155732.explanation: The CDA wants to build affordable housing for our residents, including social rental homes. However, we want to keep room to realize a portion as social homeownership (very affordable homes for purchase), so more residents have a chance at owning their own home.

155733.explanation: The CDA wants to build homes for our own residents but also preserve our valuable rural areas. For that reason, we will need to build higher in some places. The CDA wants to critically assess where this is appropriate and what height is fitting for our municipality.

155734.explanation: The CDA wants to keep space to accommodate, for example, labor migrants close to business locations. Therefore, it should sometimes be possible to convert office or commercial buildings at business parks into housing.

155735.explanation: The CDA wants to focus maximally on realizing affordable housing. If converting vacant offices with municipal subsidies contributes to this, the CDA wants to seize that opportunity and actively work on it.

155736.explanation: Due to housing shortages, some people are forced to live in holiday parks. However, parks are not designed for permanent residence and are important for tourism and the local economy. Therefore, this is not a solution. The municipality should help residents find alternatives so that no one ends up homeless.

155739.explanation: The CDA considers an accessible city center very important. Therefore, the city center of Nijkerk must also remain easily accessible by car for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

155741.explanation: The CDA finds it important that especially children learn where our food comes from. The CDA stands for the strength of society and believes that farmers and, for example, schools can organize this very well themselves without using public money.

155745.explanation: The CDA finds it important that newcomers enter the workforce as soon as possible. This promotes integration. At the same time, it helps reduce staff shortages in companies in our municipality, which the CDA views positively.

155746.explanation: Due to understaffing in the police, the CDA finds it important that enforcement officers (BOAs) can also operate safely and effectively in cases of nuisance and crime. If bodycams, batons, and/or pepper spray help with this, the CDA wants to make that possible.

155747.explanation: Nuisance has a great impact on a neighborhood. The CDA wants a firm approach. In cases of persistent and serious nuisance, the CDA believes a smart mosquito device can be used as a means to restore peace and safety for residents. This is in addition to approaches involving youth workers, police, and enforcement.

155751.explanation: The CDA stands for strong families. Many problems, including with children, originate in the family. The CDA wants to address problems at their source. If couples therapy can help reduce tensions in families, this should be made possible as municipal support.

155752.explanation: Nothing is free. Revenue from paid parking is used to maintain facilities such as parking garages. Two hours of free parking means that taxes (property tax) would have to be raised for everyone to maintain these facilities. The CDA does not want that.

155753.explanation: The planned housing development on the east side of Nijkerk and the arrival of a barracks in Zeewolde make it necessary to properly connect the east side of Nijkerk. However, this connection must be designed so that the impact on the landscape is minimal and that it does not attract unnecessary traffic.

155754.explanation: The CDA considers it important that you can park your bike well and safely in the city center of Nijkerk. Therefore, a good bicycle parking facility is needed. If this can be secured cost-effectively and user-friendly, the CDA is open to supporting it.

155756.explanation: Community gardens and collective façade or rooftop gardens strengthen togetherness, meeting, and caring for each other. The CDA wants to encourage initiatives that increase community spirit. Therefore, we want to support good plans with municipal contributions where needed.

155757.explanation: Disposing of many types of waste at the recycling center is already free. Bulky waste is not, because processing it costs relatively much money. If we make this free twice a year, it must be paid for elsewhere, leading to higher municipal taxes. The CDA does not want that.

155758.explanation: The CDA believes there is no place for wolves in our municipality. Several incidents involving wolves, such as the killing of sheep, have occurred here. Therefore, the municipality should fully commit to managing the wolf population.

155759.explanation: The CDA considers safe traffic a priority. Therefore, enforcement officers (BOAs) must strictly address nuisance and dangerous behavior with fatbikes so that pedestrians and other road users feel safe in neighborhoods and centers.

155764.explanation: The CDA sees small wind turbines for farmers as a good idea, but not large wind turbines in a direct living environment. The CDA wants to assess this case by case. In any case, placement is not possible if it leads to negative health effects for our residents.

155765.explanation: The CDA finds inclusion very important. For example, that socio-cultural events are also accessible to people with disabilities. The CDA considers a 'diversity test' for events and initiatives a step too far.

155766.explanation: The CDA considers safety in our municipality a priority. Due to police understaffing, the role of enforcement officers (BOAs) is becoming increasingly important. Therefore, the CDA wants to deploy enough BOAs to maintain supervision, enforcement, and visibility in neighborhoods.

155767.explanation: The CDA wants to preserve our rural area and not install solar panels and wind turbines everywhere. Therefore, other forms of energy generation are also needed. The CDA also wants to consider nuclear energy. It is a clean and reliable source that can contribute to a good energy mix.

155709.explanation: The municipal council is an elected body that represents the interests of all residents. This applies to all (!) subjects, both big and small, more or less important.

155710.explanation: Management of public sports facilities requires structurally professional management. It is questionable whether this can be sustainably guaranteed by resident initiatives. This requires further investigation. If it turns out that quality and funding can be guaranteed, we are open to this.

155712.explanation: The level of property tax depends on: 1. the contribution from the national government, 2. the level of municipal services. Nijkerk currently has one of the lowest housing costs in the Netherlands. We want to keep it that way, but adjusting property tax is negotiable if necessary.

155713.explanation: Thanks to various cooperation partnerships like Regio Amersfoort and Regio FoodValley, efficiency and purchasing advantages are possible. Furthermore, in relation to the national government and national subsidies, we no longer stand alone as a municipality but collaborate with other municipalities within these partnerships.

155714.explanation: Revenues from the dog tax are used to finance amenities such as dog walking areas and waste bins.

155715.explanation: To keep the balance between residents and jobs 'healthy,' an active municipal stance is necessary, especially if we want to guide the types of companies we attract.

155716.explanation: The current business parks are insufficient for all ambitions.

155717.explanation: The municipality's responsibility is not only to create a good business climate for the hospitality sector but also to ensure safety and livability in its surroundings.

155719.explanation: The municipality wants to become more sustainable and actively involve residents. By providing financial support for joint energy initiatives, more local sustainable projects get off the ground. This increases support because residents become co-owners. This also makes participation possible for residents with less financial means.

155721.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit are status holders and are given priority, as are other specific target groups. This assignment is national policy.

155722.explanation: Currently, the financial resources from the COA are sufficient to enable newcomers to integrate into society. However, the CU-SGP believes that the municipality must continue to make significant efforts to promote the integration of newcomers, but this does not have to cost extra money.

155725.explanation: That is good for social cohesion in the neighborhood. If residents of a neighborhood request this, the municipality can facilitate it appropriately.

155728.explanation: We are against the establishment of brothels, coffeeshops, or similar venues in the municipality.

155730.explanation: The municipality bears a great responsibility for the sense of safety of all residents. But safety and 'feeling safe' is not something the municipality can achieve alone. More camera surveillance is not the only solution.

155731.explanation: The current housing market makes it almost impossible for first-time buyers to enter the housing market. We believe it is important to offer first-time buyers an extra opportunity to still gain access to the housing market.

155732.explanation: We are fully committed to appropriate housing construction. Suitable for different ages, suitable for different incomes, and fitting the scale of our municipality.

155733.explanation: More high-rise apartments are not desirable. High-rise buildings can only be built at locations specified in the established environmental vision. For buildings over 20 meters (14 meters in Nijkerkerveen), municipal council approval is always required.

155734.explanation: We believe that business parks are designated for companies that contribute to a vital local economy.

155735.explanation: We believe it should be possible to convert vacant offices into housing. The initiative must come from the owner/developer. Municipal subsidies do not apply in this case.

155736.explanation: Holiday parks are not designed for permanent residence regarding facilities and access roads. We want to prevent holiday homes from being permanently inhabited as a temporary solution for the tight housing market.

155739.explanation: We want to encourage walking and cycling. However, the city center must definitely remain accessible by car.

155741.explanation: We choose for a vital countryside. Farmers provide food and manage the landscape. Events like open farm days contribute to connection and mutual understanding between society and the agricultural sector.

155745.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality; however, the municipality can stimulate the local business community to guide people towards employment.

155746.explanation: Aggression against enforcement officers is increasing, including in Nijkerk. To ensure their safety and enable them to continue their work properly, enforcement officers must have appropriate equipment. Therefore, the CU-SGP supports allowing the use of batons alongside other supportive tools.

155747.explanation: It has been proven that using smart mosquitos is effective against loitering youth causing nuisance. However, displacement of the nuisance must be prevented.

155751.explanation: Couples therapy can help prevent divorces or relationship problems that may lead to high societal costs, such as mental healthcare or youth care expenses.

155752.explanation: Visitors to Nijkerk's center indicate they find the city center attractive and perceive the parking fees as low. Therefore, this measure will have little or no impact on the center's attractiveness but will lead to extra costs for the municipality.

155753.explanation: We want a future-proof route that provides access to residential neighborhoods and space for housing development.

155754.explanation: We encourage cycling to the center. This calls for a bicycle parking facility. If there is enough demand for a guarded bicycle parking, a commercial party, possibly with municipal support, could provide this.

155756.explanation: That is good for the livability and social cohesion in the neighborhood. If residents of a neighborhood request this, the municipality can facilitate it appropriately.

155757.explanation: This reduces the chance of illegal dumping and also combats littering and clutter. It is also a great addition to the measure of no longer issuing large (240-liter) bins.

155758.explanation: The municipality must take responsibility if a problem wolf is present within or around the municipality of Nijkerk.

155759.explanation: Riders of fatbikes especially cause nuisance and unsafe situations in the center. We consider this unacceptable and it must be enforced.

155764.explanation: We do not want to exclude any form of sustainable (non-fossil) energy in advance, including wind energy. The health and livability of our residents are paramount.

155765.explanation: The municipality must consider diversity and inclusion.

155766.explanation: Enforcement officers already handle (social) safety for a large part of their duties. In our view, this can be expanded and carried out more preventively. We place great importance on promoting safety.

155767.explanation: We do not want to exclude any form of non-fossil energy generation in advance.

155709.explanation: Currently, the municipality has a referendum regulation. PRO21 considers it important that this option exists. PRO21 also finds it important that realistic expectations are set for residents in this regard.

155710.explanation: Good sports facilities are important. Managing them requires much time and attention. This demands a lot from volunteers, so it is not realistic to expect this from residents alone.

155712.explanation: We strive for a good level of facilities, appropriate for our residents. It may be that the costs exceed the budget. We believe it is important to weigh whether budget cuts or generating additional income are necessary. Our starting point is: ability to pay.

155713.explanation: The principle should not be that the municipality manages as much as possible itself, but that tasks are executed as well and efficiently as possible. In some cases, this means cooperating with other municipalities, without compromising our own influence. Less cooperation would weaken rather than strengthen the municipality.

155714.explanation: A condition for retaining this tax is that the municipality uses the revenue from this tax for dog owners. This includes, among other things, waste bins and off-leash areas.

155715.explanation: First, it must be determined whether more companies are needed for employment. With the arrival of the barracks, many jobs may be created. PRO21 wants to first focus on the current opportunities.

155716.explanation: Nijkerk must keep its business parks attractive. Expansion is not the main focus. First, we can look at good planning, taking into account housing and business owners to improve business activities.

155717.explanation: PRO21 believes entrepreneurs need flexibility, but also that the interests of neighbors and the livability of the city matter. Therefore, a joint arrangement between the municipality and entrepreneurs can provide tailor-made solutions.

155719.explanation: The energy transition must be shared fairly and justly. The municipality has a role to support and contribute ideas; this does not always have to be through financial assistance.

155721.explanation: A home helps with integration. Giving priority may seem unfair, but ultimately helps everyone in the municipality of Nijkerk. Moreover, PRO21 finds it important not only to give priority to status holders but to look more broadly at people in vulnerable positions.

155722.explanation: PRO21 wants to invest in diversity and inclusion. Meeting each other is an important part of this. This also helps with integration.

155725.explanation: PRO21 is committed to improving green maintenance. If residents can do this better themselves, the municipality should support such initiatives.

155728.explanation: We do not want to encourage drug use. Therefore, in our view, no coffeeshop will be established.

155730.explanation: Camera surveillance should be a last resort. If security and supervision really cannot be achieved otherwise, camera surveillance is an option.

155731.explanation: The housing market is becoming increasingly difficult. To support young people, PRO21 wants the starter loan to remain available.

155732.explanation: PRO21 wants everyone to have the opportunity to secure housing. The national target is 30%. Nijkerk is currently below that. If we want to meet the national target, PRO21 wants to agree on a higher number to reach that 30%.

155733.explanation: There are too few homes. Therefore, we must build more housing quickly. If high-rise buildings in suitable locations, like Stadshaven in Nijkerk, allow us to build fewer homes in green areas while meeting local housing needs, that is PRO21's preferred choice.

155734.explanation: PRO21 considers a livable residential environment important. An industrial estate is not always a livable residential environment. PRO21 does not rule it out in advance, but does not think it should happen.

155735.explanation: A livable residential environment is an important condition for PRO21. Vacancy is a waste, so the municipality can engage in discussions to convert vacant properties into homes, and this does not have to involve financial support.

155736.explanation: Having a permanent residence in a holiday park can lead to unsafe situations. A holiday park is meant for recreation, not living. People currently staying there long-term should be guided to permanent housing.

155739.explanation: The city center must remain easily accessible for residents, entrepreneurs, visitors, and people dependent on cars, for example for shopping or healthcare. At the same time, the center can be arranged to limit through car traffic, making it more pleasant, safer, and more attractive to stay. In this way, accessibility and livability strengthen each other.

155741.explanation: Education is important. If everyone, especially young children, knows where food comes from, they will also appreciate it more.

155745.explanation: The municipality does not control the hiring policies of local entrepreneurs.

155746.explanation: PRO21 chooses prevention. Enforcement officers fulfill a supervisory and connecting role.

155747.explanation: PRO21 stands for openness, honesty, and closeness. That means we commit to working with young people to find solutions that reduce nuisance.

155751.explanation: The relationship between two people is a matter for the couple. The municipality already offers certain possibilities, such as a buddy program.

155752.explanation: An attractive city center should have a beautiful appearance. PRO21 promotes cycling and walking to and within the center. Drivers pay a fair rate as users of parking facilities.

155753.explanation: PRO21 wants more homes to be built that are accessible by public transport, bicycle, and car. Therefore, we will establish a mobility vision. This will describe how we manage traffic throughout the municipality of Nijkerk. The construction of an eastern access road is part of this.

155754.explanation: There must be sufficient and safe bicycle parking in the center of Nijkerk. A guarded bicycle parking facility can contribute to this and makes it more attractive to use the bike. If such a facility also provides opportunities for people with barriers to the labor market, that is a great added value.

155756.explanation: There is increasing rainfall and expanding sewers is very expensive. Plants absorb water and provide cooling and oxygen. Therefore, PRO21 wants the municipality to allocate funds.

155757.explanation: PRO21 supports the principle 'the polluter pays.' The goal is to have less waste. That is why we support paying for bringing waste to the recycling center, encouraging people to think about reusing items.

155758.explanation: PRO21 believes that wolf deterrent measures should be chosen.

155759.explanation: Nuisance must be addressed. PRO21 believes it should be done in the most effective way. Stricter enforcement is not always effective. Enforcement officers must be able to choose what is best for the individual and society. Campaigns and education can also help.

155764.explanation: PRO21 wants the municipality to take the lead in creating a clean, green, and healthy living environment. All health risks must be considered when making choices. For PRO21, it is important that the benefits flow back to the residents of the municipality of Nijkerk.

155765.explanation: In Nijkerk, everyone counts. PRO21 advocates subsidies so that everyone can participate.

155766.explanation: Nijkerk has grown and has more residents. Therefore, more enforcement officers are important to ensure safety.

155767.explanation: PRO21 does not see nuclear energy as the energy source of the future.

155709.explanation: The most important point is that the topic must be suitable for a referendum and that no false hopes are raised. But citizen participation is very important to The Local Party. Participation at an early stage is even more important to The Local Party.

155710.explanation: The Local Party believes this contributes to both the quality of service and involvement and accessibility of the facility. Operating a sports hall is not a municipal task. If local residents want to do this, The Local Party is positive about it.

155712.explanation: First, it should be examined whether savings can be made elsewhere or if there are other creative ideas. The Local Party believes that property tax must not become a cash cow to cover other expenses. Recently, more subsidies have also been requested from the national and provincial governments.

155713.explanation: The Local Party believes that municipal implementation activities should be as close to residents as possible. That means doing what you can locally. Regional cooperation is justified if it leads to lower costs for our residents or better services.

155714.explanation: In 2025, 113 of the 342 municipalities will still levy a dog tax. This number is steadily decreasing. The costs of administration and control no longer outweigh the revenue of €145,600. In short: abolish it as two-thirds of the Netherlands already has.

155715.explanation: Currently, the Municipality of Nijkerk has little industrial estate available. The Local Party wants to designate this mainly for local entrepreneurs who want to expand. Growth for neighboring municipalities does not fit our vision.

155716.explanation: Expansion on a limited scale only for entrepreneurs who already have their business in Nijkerk. No growth for the region.

155717.explanation: The Local Party fully supports this, with the supervisory role lying with the entrepreneurs. They must ensure there is no nuisance for neighbors outside their hospitality businesses.

155719.explanation: The national government offers various options for compensation in acquisition or exemption from VAT. As a municipality, we provide loans at a lower interest rate. We should continue with this.

155721.explanation: The Local Party notes there is a large shortage of housing for our own residents. To avoid increasing pressure on the housing market, The Local Party agrees with current policy but definitely does not want expansion.

155722.explanation: This is primarily a national responsibility that Nijkerk carries out very well and for which it also receives 100% compensation from the national government. No additional municipal contributions are necessary. If more is required, it should be fully funded by the national government.

155725.explanation: The Local Party agrees. Residents who want to maintain greenery in their neighborhood themselves deserve support from the municipality. By allocating funds for this, we stimulate involvement, strengthen livability, and together keep our neighborhoods green and tidy.

155728.explanation: Setting up coffeeshops does not fit the character and scale of our municipality. We are concerned about possible nuisance, safety risks, and attracting drug tourism. In addition, we believe prevention and care deserve priority over facilitating sales.

155730.explanation: Camera surveillance is important for residents' safety and preventing vandalism. The municipality of Nijkerk must use this purposefully, with respect for privacy, so it contributes both preventively and in enforcement to a safer living environment.

155731.explanation: The starter loan in Nijkerk must remain available for people buying a home for the first time. It works well and helps starters to take the step to home ownership. It is important that more and clearer information is provided so that everyone can make optimal use of it.

155732.explanation: The current policy in Nijkerk requires that at least 35% of newly built homes be social rental housing. With only about 23% social rental housing in the municipality, it is important to maintain this policy to ensure sufficient affordable housing remains available for starters and lower incomes.

155733.explanation: More high-rise apartments in Nijkerk are sensible: they help solve the housing shortage, make efficient use of limited space, and prevent construction in green areas. This way, we can grow without unnecessarily damaging nature and the living environment.

155734.explanation: Living on industrial estates can help reduce the housing shortage and efficiently utilize existing space. It is important that safety, noise, and environmental issues are well regulated so that living and working do not interfere with each other and the quality of both functions is maintained.

155735.explanation: Vacant offices can be converted into homes without needing subsidies, but with municipal facilitation where necessary. This allows us to efficiently use existing buildings, help solve the housing shortage, and save green and open spaces.

155736.explanation: The current policy in Nijkerk does not allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residential address. We adhere to national legislation on this. Using them as permanent residences does not fit within the municipality’s existing rules and spatial plans.

155739.explanation: The city center of Nijkerk must remain easily accessible by car. This is important for entrepreneurs and ensures that parking remains affordable, with the first two hours free for visitors, keeping the center attractive and economically vital.

155741.explanation: We agree that the municipality should invest in open farm days to show residents where their food comes from. This can be done through the existing one-time subsidy and is also beneficial for local businesses and the regional economy.

155745.explanation: Refugees with residence permits must find work themselves in Nijkerk. The municipality can facilitate and support, for example through information and network contacts, but it is not the municipality’s task or obligation to guarantee employment.

155746.explanation: We support the idea that enforcement officers (boa’s) overseeing safety in Nijkerk be equipped with bodycams, batons, and pepper spray. This increases their safety and enforcement capability, makes their work more effective, and enhances residents' trust in public order.

155747.explanation: We view the principle of smart mosquitos positively to reduce nuisance caused by hanging out youth. It is important first to engage in dialogue with young people and parents, and only then take additional measures, ensuring solutions are effective, fair, and have broad support.

155751.explanation: We agree that relationship therapy can be reimbursed, but only in a pilot program, primarily for parents with children. Because this is not covered by basic insurance, it must be offered in a limited and targeted manner to strengthen relationships and prevent negative effects on children.

155752.explanation: The Local Party wants the first two hours of parking in the center of Nijkerk to be free. This encourages local shopping, increases accessibility, and ensures a lively center, benefiting residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

155753.explanation: We are positive about an eastern bypass road in Nijkerk, provided that residents’ input is central and nature and the living environment are well protected. It is important that the road is designed to prevent shortcut traffic and that financing from the province and national government is secured.

155754.explanation: The Local Party agrees: a monitored bicycle parking facility in the center of Nijkerk increases safety, protects bikes from theft and vandalism, and makes it more attractive for residents and visitors to cycle to the center sustainably and without worries.

155756.explanation: We find the green roof scheme in Nijkerk good and effective. There is currently no specific subsidy for neighborhood and facade gardens, but perhaps this can be facilitated in the future through neighborhood maintenance, enabling residents to green and make their environment more sustainable.

155757.explanation: The Local Party believes residents should be able to dispose of waste for free twice a year at the recycling center. This encourages correct waste separation, prevents illegal dumping, and makes it easier for residents to keep their neighborhood clean and livable.

155758.explanation: The Local Party agrees: if a wolf causes problems, the municipality should urge the province to authorize culling. This protects residents, farm animals, and ensures a safe balance between nature and the living environment.

155759.explanation: The Local Party believes enforcers (boa’s) should more strictly tackle nuisance caused by fatbikes. This protects pedestrians, children, and the elderly, reduces dangerous situations, and ensures a safe and livable municipality without disturbance from unsafe or excessive fatbike use.

155764.explanation: In practice, the municipality sees that the national government sets increasingly higher requirements, making wind turbines in Nijkerk nearly impossible. We support sustainable energy, but never in residential areas or places where residents suffer from shadow flicker or noise. That would only be possible along the A28.

155765.explanation: We embrace the principle that diversity and inclusion be considered in socio-cultural subsidies. This must happen realistically and balanced, so associations are encouraged to work inclusively without compromising their capacity and activities.

155766.explanation: We strongly support deploying more municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) in Nijkerk. They should be given more authority and be visible throughout the municipality, so safety increases, nuisance decreases, and residents feel safer.

155767.explanation: The municipality of Nijkerk focuses on sustainable energy such as solar and wind and has no policy for nuclear energy. We believe the focus locally should remain on renewable and safe energy sources that fit the municipality and have residents’ support.

155709.explanation: The VVD chooses that residents can co-decide on this, but the question must be clearly answerable with ‘yes’ or ‘no’. The general interest must always take precedence over individual interests.

155710.explanation: The VVD chooses certainty, but when a good initiative comes from residents, space should be provided for it.

155712.explanation: The VVD maintains provisions at a good level but remains prudent with costs. The municipality of Nijkerk must remain affordable for its residents.

155713.explanation: The VVD chooses local where possible: we carry out tasks ourselves; where cooperation is needed, we cooperate. Legally required collaborations, such as the safety region and FoodValley Youth, remain, but local implementation is preferred.

155714.explanation: The VVD believes that dog tax should be used to address nuisance caused by dog feces. Only when that nuisance has disappeared can the tax be abolished.

155715.explanation: The VVD wants Nijkerk and Hoevelaken to be attractive for businesses and to offer plenty of jobs for residents, with a good business climate. However, it is not necessary for the municipality to spend tax money on a promotional campaign.

155716.explanation: The VVD chooses certainty; more homes are needed, but if more people live in Nijkerk, they must also be able to play sports and work locally. This also requires expansion of business parks.

155717.explanation: The VVD believes entrepreneurs should have space to do business, with as few rules as possible, but with conditions to limit nuisance to the surroundings.

155719.explanation: The VVD is fine with the municipality allowing and supporting such initiatives, but it seems unfair to us to use tax money from all Nijkerk residents for this.

155721.explanation: The VVD believes our children should no longer have to wait for a house. It is good that the municipality uses transition locations for status holders, but they do not get priority on the regular housing market.

155722.explanation: The VVD sees immigration as a national issue. Residents can certainly contribute to integration, and we do so through volunteer work. Additionally, the municipality establishes a 'participation' development center, contributing to integration. This is financed from national government contributions, as it should be.

155725.explanation: The VVD chooses that this is well arranged; if residents have a good initiative, space should be provided for it.

155728.explanation: The VVD is not in favor of drug use. It is known that the safety around coffee shops is under pressure.

155730.explanation: The VVD chooses yes, provided residents’ privacy is well safeguarded. It is important that new measures or systems are implemented carefully to promote safety and efficiency without infringing on personal data.

155731.explanation: The VVD supports this: this scheme is successful and costs the municipality almost nothing because the loans are repaid and can be reused. This way, we can help more starters buy their own home.

155732.explanation: This is already policy endorsed by the VVD and the municipal council. The VVD supports this policy and sees it as a valuable way to support the municipality’s goals.

155733.explanation: The VVD finds this difficult: we want more homes for our children, more space for nature, businesses, sports clubs, and our farmers. To realize this, we sometimes have to make choices, for example by building higher in locations where this fits.

155734.explanation: The VVD believes a business park should live up to its name. Building homes on such locations is often difficult due to odor, noise nuisance, or emission of unhealthy substances. Entrepreneurs should not be limited in their business operations by housing on industrial estates.

155735.explanation: The VVD believes Nijkerk must have enough companies to ensure jobs for residents. Sometimes it can be useful to convert offices into homes, but the municipality does not need to pay for this; the owner or developer can do that themselves.

155736.explanation: The law currently does not allow this either. Therefore, we must protect recreation and not permit new residential situations. Temporary occupancy during, for example, renovations is allowed.

155739.explanation: The VVD sees this as self-evident: our entrepreneurs must be able to properly receive their guests, and this requires good infrastructure.

155741.explanation: The VVD believes that no tax money needs to be made available for this. It is fine if the municipality supports this, for instance by helping with the organization or coordination of cultural activities.

155745.explanation: The VVD believes it is not the municipality’s task to provide status holders with a job; that responsibility lies with entrepreneurs. The municipality can mediate and show which companies are open to cooperation so that status holders can participate in society faster.

155746.explanation: The VVD advocates for more police on the streets and in neighborhoods, but due to a shortage of officers, the deployment of municipal enforcement officers (boa's) is necessary. Their safety is important, so we want to equip our boa's with batons, pepper spray, and body cams so they are well equipped to contribute to safety in our municipality.

155747.explanation: Nuisance must be prevented. Therefore, the VVD chooses to use mosquito devices in places with a lot of nuisance. This should go hand in hand with working together with young people and guiding them so they do not cause nuisance and develop positively in the neighborhood. This way, we prevent the problem from shifting.

155751.explanation: The VVD believes the minister determines what health insurers reimburse, not the municipality. Therefore, municipal tax money does not need to be used for this.

155752.explanation: The VVD considers proper parking arrangements important, but free parking does not exist. After all, facilities need to be maintained. It is unfair for residents who do not use parking to contribute via municipal taxes.

155753.explanation: The VVD finds this absolutely necessary. The current council is already committed to this, and the next council will continue to do so, regardless of its composition.

155754.explanation: The VVD believes it is high time for good bicycle parking facilities. Proper and safe bicycle parking encourages the use of bicycles, relieves traffic, and contributes to an attractive and livable center for residents and visitors.

155756.explanation: The VVD believes the municipality can facilitate and possibly contribute financially to good ideas, but mostly the responsibility lies with the initiators themselves. The taxpayer cannot pay for everything. We stand for low taxes and personal responsibility.

155757.explanation: There is no such thing as free waste: residents pay for this via waste charges. Still, the VVD supports an accessible and low-threshold system to encourage waste separation and prevent waste from being dumped anywhere. This may seem 'free,' but it does lead to higher waste charges.

155758.explanation: The VVD believes the wolf does not belong in Nijkerk. Although we value nature conservation, the risks for residents, animals, and agriculture weigh heavily. Safety and livability in our municipality come first, while we continue to keep an eye on nature and biodiversity.

155759.explanation: The VVD believes that not only municipal enforcement officers but also the police must be more visible in neighborhoods and on the streets. This increases safety, enhances residents' feeling of security, and makes it easier to act quickly when needed.

155764.explanation: Our electricity grid is full, so houses and businesses cannot get a power connection. All solutions are welcome, provided they pose no health risks. Small wind turbines can be a temporary measure.

155765.explanation: The VVD values diversity and inclusion, but it cannot be a requirement for every subsidy. Traditional activities also deserve respect and can be supported as long as they contribute to a vibrant and connected society.

155766.explanation: The VVD considers more municipal enforcement officers important to increase safety in Nijkerk. Thanks in part to VVD efforts, this is already being provided for. Extra enforcement officers ensure visible presence in neighborhoods, faster responses to incidents, and a safer feeling for all residents.

155767.explanation: The VVD supports the use of nuclear energy. Modern plants are much safer, produce hardly any waste, and supply large amounts of clean energy. This is important for our entrepreneurs, to continue building houses, and to make Nijkerk as sustainable as possible.

155709.explanation: A referendum can, under good conditions, strengthen local democracy.

155710.explanation: Residents can take over management but not everywhere or always. The municipality must make clear agreements and continue monitoring safety, costs, and accessibility.

155712.explanation: Taxes are important for the proper functioning of the municipality. They ensure there is money for roads, schools, sports, safety, and other facilities. Taxes must be fair and clear. Everyone contributes, even in difficult times. This keeps the municipality financially healthy.

155713.explanation: Collaborations can offer advantages and strengthen efforts. But important decisions must be made by the municipality of Nijkerk itself. This way, the council and residents retain control over what happens in their municipality.

155714.explanation: Dog tax may be abolished, as it is an extra burden for residents. Many residents find the tax unfair. Abolishing it saves administrative costs and makes the rules clearer and fairer.

155715.explanation: Our municipality is growing. More and more people live here. That means more jobs and more businesses are needed. Entrepreneurs provide jobs, lively villages, and events. Therefore, we want entrepreneurs to have space to grow and for new companies to settle here.

155716.explanation: The municipality of Nijkerk is growing. To provide employment, entrepreneurs need space. Successful business parks show that growth works. New business parks are important.

155717.explanation: Unlimited freedom can lead to nuisance for neighbors and extra deployment of police and enforcement. We find it important that entrepreneurs have freedom, but always with consideration for safety, livability, and support in the neighborhood.

155719.explanation: Encouraging, for example, home and neighborhood batteries increases the use of locally generated green energy, lowers energy costs, stabilizes the grid, and makes energy more accessible to everyone, even without solar panels, thereby increasing sustainable energy and energy independence.

155721.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit must be housed according to the national tasking. As soon as the law allows, a distribution based on registration duration can be implemented, where a refugee starts with 5 registration years. This gives both newcomers and residents a fair chance.

155722.explanation: Integration of newcomers must be supported, provided it is effective and targeted. We focus on language, participation, and work so funds benefit newcomers who want to integrate.

155725.explanation: No standard funds need to be made available for residents who want to maintain greenery themselves. However, initiatives can be supported through existing funds and options so residents can actively contribute to their neighborhood without extra costs.

155728.explanation: The establishment of coffeeshops should not be routinely permitted because this can lead to nuisance, safety risks, and undesirable situations. Drug use in the municipality should be actively countered in all forms.

155730.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety and reducing nuisance. The preference is for targeted deployment of mobile cameras at locations where problems occur, with attention to privacy.

155731.explanation: The starter’s loan helps first-time buyers in the municipality of Nijkerk purchase their first home in a tight housing market. It increases opportunities for residents to continue living in their own municipality.

155732.explanation: By realizing 35% of new homes as social rental housing, we address the growing need for affordable housing in the municipality of Nijkerk. Starters and people with lower incomes get more opportunities without losing sight of local differences.

155733.explanation: High-rise apartment buildings can help with housing supply and densification in the municipality of Nijkerk, provided they fit the size and scale of the core, so livability and character of the environment are preserved.

155734.explanation: Business parks may also allow company residences, so entrepreneurs and employees can live close to their work, which supports efficient space use and benefits business operations.

155735.explanation: Converting vacant offices into homes makes use of existing buildings. This adds housing and helps combat vacancy in the municipality of Nijkerk. This benefits starters and residents with middle incomes.

155736.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation. Allowing them as permanent residences can cause legal, fiscal, and infrastructure problems and disrupt the balance between recreation and housing.

155739.explanation: The inner city of Nijkerk does not need to be accessible to all cars; however, accessibility for suppliers must be guaranteed to allow shops and facilities to function well. There is sufficient parking for visitors.

155741.explanation: The municipality does not need to invest money here. Farmers can organize open farm days themselves, and municipal funds are better spent on local priorities such as safety, maintenance, and facilities.

155745.explanation: Offering jobs is not a task for the municipality. Integration works better through the regular labor market and employers. The municipality can provide support with guidance and information.

155746.explanation: This increases the safety of enforcement officers (boa's). Bodycams provide evidence during incidents and ensure transparency; a baton and pepper spray give boa's defensive options. The core task of boa's remains enforcement through prevention and communication.

155747.explanation: The municipality should not drive away young people. They should be given opportunities to do something and have a place to meet. Enforcement should be applied where necessary.

155751.explanation: Couples therapy is a private matter and not a core task of the municipality. Municipal funds should be spent on necessary care and support for residents who cannot provide it themselves.

155752.explanation: Free parking in the city center helps local businesses. Two hours is ambitious; one hour free followed by a higher rate fits better and aligns with neighboring towns where parking is free.

155753.explanation: A bypass on the east side of Nijkerk must not run through Slichtenhorst. The area is a valuable jewel that must be preserved. An alternative must be considered.

155754.explanation: This is already planned for the center of Nijkerk. A supervised bicycle parking near Hoevelaken station would also be an improvement.

155756.explanation: It is not a core task of the municipality to finance individual neighborhood projects. Residents can organize this themselves through subsidies and funds.

155757.explanation: Free waste disposal encourages correct waste separation, prevents illegal dumping, and makes it accessible for residents. Paying per disposal encourages conscious use. Free disposal increases municipal costs, so one free disposal is sympathetic and sufficient.

155758.explanation: The municipality must be able to act if a wolf poses a danger, so people remain protected. Prevention and respect for nature remain important.

155759.explanation: If fatbike riders cause nuisance, stricter enforcement is required. Stricter enforcement protects safety, livability, and enjoyment of public spaces.

155764.explanation: Disadvantages such as impaired views, noise pollution, and shadows must be limited.

155765.explanation: Everyone participates: with every socio-cultural subsidy, the municipality must promote diversity and inclusion. This ensures projects are accessible to all residents and everyone can participate.

155766.explanation: Extra supervision ensures better compliance with rules, faster tackling of nuisance, violations, and crime, and residents feel safer in their park, neighborhood, or center. In addition, boa's can act preventively and contribute to social control and livability.

155767.explanation: Nuclear energy provides reliable, low-CO2 power and helps achieve climate goals. Investing or cooperating can ensure safe, efficient energy supply and prevent the municipality from depending on unstable energy sources.

155709.explanation: FVD supports direct democracy and believes residents should have more say in important decisions. Referenda give residents direct influence on policies affecting them, reduce the gap between politics and society, and strengthen democratic legitimacy.

155710.explanation: FVD believes residents should have more control over their living environment. The right to challenge allows residents to take over municipal tasks, such as managing a sports hall, when they think it can be done better.

155712.explanation: FVD opposes higher taxes, such as property tax (ozb). The municipality should finance social services by working more efficiently and prioritizing better, or by reallocating funds from other policy areas—not by taxing residents extra.

155713.explanation: FVD wants the municipality to make its own decisions and remain sovereign. Too many regional collaborations increase distance from residents and reduce democratic oversight.

155714.explanation: FVD wants to abolish the dog tax. This tax is seen as unnecessary and unfair because there is no clear service in return. It is an extra burden for residents and does not fit a municipality that is prudent and efficient with money.

155715.explanation: FVD opposes spending tax money on promotional campaigns. A good business climate arises from low charges and fewer rules, not from municipal marketing.

155716.explanation: FVD wants to better utilize existing industrial sites first. Expansion is only an option when truly necessary and not at the expense of the living environment.

155717.explanation: FVD wants to give entrepreneurs more freedom. Cafés and restaurants should be able to set their own opening hours without unnecessary municipal restrictions.

155719.explanation: Residents may generate their own energy, but the municipality should not subsidize this. It is not a core government task to finance sustainable projects, and this leads to higher burdens for residents. Energy choices should be free and made without tax money.

155721.explanation: Social rental homes are scarce. Residents who have been waiting for years should not be displaced by priority for status holders. The municipality must first cater to its own residents and allocate housing based on equal rules for everyone.

155722.explanation: FVD believes integration should not cost extra municipal tax money; newcomers themselves are responsible for their integration process.

155725.explanation: Citizen initiatives are fine but without financial incentives. Maintenance of public greenery is a municipal task and should not involve extra tax money or subsidies.

155728.explanation: As long as certain conditions are met, such as safety and impact on neighbors, a coffeeshop should be allowed to open in the municipality.

155730.explanation: Camera surveillance helps counteract nuisance and crime and supports the police in investigations. At the same time, the party believes residents' privacy must be safeguarded: no tracking systems, no automatic recognition, and no digital 'smart city' experiments.

155731.explanation: Even though the scheme targets local residents, starter loans distort the housing market and drive up prices. The municipality should not spend extra money on loans but ensure more building space and fewer regulations so that starters truly get opportunities.

155732.explanation: Mandatory social rental quotas make building more expensive because projects become less profitable and costs are passed on to other homes. The municipality should reduce rules so that homes become available for all groups—including starters and middle incomes.

155733.explanation: High apartment buildings do not fit the character of Nijkerk. The municipality must opt for homes that match the local identity so that Nijkerk retains its own character and is not further urbanized.

155734.explanation: Industrial sites are meant for businesses and employment.

155735.explanation: Converting offices can be useful, but not with tax money. Subsidies distort the market and are not a core municipal task. Projects must be financially viable on their own.

155736.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, the municipality must be flexible with available space. If holiday homes are suitable and recreation is not hindered, temporary habitation should be allowed under clear conditions.

155739.explanation: The city center must remain easily accessible for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

155741.explanation: FVD believes it is important to portray farmers positively. At the same time, the party is cautious about municipal subsidies. The municipality should mainly accommodate farmers by reducing regulatory pressure so they have the space to organize such activities themselves.

155745.explanation: According to FVD, local businesses must decide for themselves whom they hire.

155746.explanation: The monopoly on violence lies with the police and must remain there. The municipality must ensure police capacity is increased locally, not assign police tasks to boa's.

155747.explanation: Mosquito systems punish all young people equally. Nuisance must be tackled with visible enforcement. FVD is critical of technological coercive means.

155751.explanation: FVD values prevention, investing in strong families and good basic facilities, not reimbursing private therapy.

155752.explanation: Free parking for the first two hours supports residents and entrepreneurs without additional costs.

155753.explanation: Improved traffic flow is important, but not at the expense of the landscape and environmental character of Nijkerk. Traffic improvements should be sought through alternatives that respect these qualities.

155754.explanation: A supervised bicycle parking facility makes the city center safer, clearer, and more attractive for residents and visitors.

155756.explanation: Neighborhood and facade gardens are great initiatives but not a municipal task. FVD does not support climate subsidies. Tax money should not go to private garden projects.

155757.explanation: Free disposal moments keep the municipality clean and prevent illegal dumping.

155758.explanation: The safety of residents and livestock comes first. For problematic wolves, the municipality should ask the province to intervene, including culling if necessary.

155759.explanation: A fatbike is simply an object for use. Nuisance and dangerous riding behavior must be addressed.

155764.explanation: Wind turbines are not profitable, cause nuisance, and damage the landscape. The municipality must protect residents from horizon pollution, noise, and shadow flicker. Also, wind turbines depend on subsidies and do not provide stable energy. Therefore, we are against them.

155765.explanation: The municipality must serve all residents. Mandatory consideration of diversity/inclusion in every socio-cultural subsidy leads to bureaucracy and exclusion of initiatives. The municipality should assess quality, usefulness, and support, not political agendas or imposed standards.

155766.explanation: Safety is primarily the task of the police. Fully qualified officers are always preferred. But more visible enforcement (including authorized officers) helps combat nuisance, vandalism, and insecurity.

155767.explanation: Modern nuclear energy is safe and a reliable, stable energy source that can contribute to energy security and affordability. A total ban on cooperation or investment is unrealistic and limits future energy options.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
At least 35% of newly built homes in Nijkerk should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Nijkerk moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more CCTV surveillance in the municipality of Nijkerk.
statements.moreInfo: Camera surveillance refers to the use of cameras in public places, such as streets and squares. Camera footage of criminal offences can be shared with the police. In Nijkerk there is already camera surveillance in some locations, for example around the Paasbos shopping centre.

statements.title: Residents who plan to create neighbourhood, façade or rooftop gardens should receive funding from the municipality.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Parking in the centre of Nijkerk should be free for the first two hours.
statements.moreInfo: Parking in certain parts of Nijkerk’s town centre now costs €1.10 per hour.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: Property tax (ozb) should be increased to maintain social services.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Services and schemes for residents, such as financial support, care, culture, sport and recreation.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a promotional campaign to market Nijkerk as an attractive location for businesses.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.

titleParts.text: voor Nijkerk als aantrekkelijke vestigingsplaats voor bedrijven.

statements.title: The municipality should never invest in or cooperate with plans for nuclear energy.
statements.moreInfo: Nuclear energy is the energy produced by splitting the atomic nuclei of the fuel uranium. The province of Gelderland is examining locations for building a small nuclear reactor that generates nuclear energy, also called a Small Modular Reactor (SMR). A possible location is an area on the Veluwemeer near Harderwijk. The province aims to decide on this by 2027 at the latest.

statements.title: The municipality should commit to building a bypass on the east side of Nijkerk.
statements.moreInfo: The future ring road will run on the right (east) side of the core of Nijkerk. There are already two plans for additional roads: one on the south side of Nijkerk (between the Arkemheenweg and the Barneveldseweg/Chopinlaan roundabout) and one on the northeast side (extending the Berencamperweg near the Vetkamp towards the Bloemendaalseweg). The future ring road will connect these two additional roads.

statements.title: Residents should be able to dispose of waste at the recycling centre for free twice a year.

titleParts.text: Inwoners moeten twee keer per jaar gratis afval kunnen storten bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality's starter loan should remain available for people buying a house for the first time.

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente moet blijven bestaan, voor mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in open farm days at farms to show residents where their food comes from.
The municipality must allow the establishment of coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: If a wolf causes problems, the municipality must urge the province to have it shot.
The municipality must open a guarded bicycle parking facility in the centre of Nijkerk.
Refugees who receive a residence permit and move to the municipality of Nijkerk should be offered a job in the local business community by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands.

titleParts.text: krijgen en in gemeente Nijkerk komen wonen, moet de gemeente een baan aanbieden in het lokale bedrijfsleven.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.moreInfo: Homeowners can install solar panels on their roof if the roof is suitable, or apply for a permit for a wind turbine in the garden if it is large enough. If that is not possible, people can buy or rent land together with others for one or more wind turbines or solar panels so they can still invest in renewable energy. Renewable energy is generated from sources that never run out, such as sunlight and wind.

statements.title: The municipality must consider diversity and inclusion as a condition for every socio-cultural subsidy.

titleParts.glossary: Aimed at bringing people together to jointly experience, for example, traditions or art.
Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.
Actively involving and treating all people equally, regardless of their background, characteristics or any disability.

statements.title: The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who monitor safety in the municipality of Nijkerk should be given a body camera, a baton and pepper spray.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in de gemeente Nijkerk controleren op veiligheid moeten een bodycam, wapenstok en pepperspray krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should handle more itself and participate less in partnerships with other municipalities.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality has many tasks. Most Dutch municipalities work together to carry out those tasks. The aldermen and civil servants then consult with each other to arrange the cooperation. A decision by such a cooperative body then applies to all municipalities participating in it.

statements.title: More tall apartment buildings should be allowed in Nijkerk.
The municipality must oppose the construction of new wind turbines everywhere.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall from the municipality.
The municipality should disperse nuisance-causing loitering youth with smart "mosquito" devices: devices that emit an annoying sound people over 25 cannot hear.
Housing should also be built on business parks.
Residents who go into couples therapy should have it reimbursed by the municipality.

shootoutStatements.title: Residents of Nijkerk should continue to be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.
The business parks in Nijkerk should be expanded.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety should be appointed.
Nijkerk’s city center should remain easily accessible by car.
Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should more strictly tackle nuisance from fatbikes in Nijkerk.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should make funds available to citizens who want to maintain the greenery in their neighborhood themselves.
